name: One Roof Community Housing
description_short: Non-profit housing organization offering homebuyer education, community land trust homes, repair loans, and tenant-landlord mediation to low-income families and individuals.
description_long:
One Roof Community Housing is a mission-driven non-profit organization dedicated to making homeownership and stable housing accessible to low-income individuals and families in their community. The organization was founded on the belief that everyone deserves a home and deserves choices in achieving it, working to address gaps in the housing market that particularly affect economically disadvantaged populations.

One Roof provides a comprehensive spectrum of housing services including homebuyer education programs, Community Land Trust (CLT) home purchases with affordability protections, down payment assistance, affordable home improvement loans at virtually any income level, a free community tool lending library, and education plus mediation services for renters and landlords. They also develop rental housing through housing developments and provide ongoing stewardship resources specifically for CLT homeowners to support their long-term success.

What distinguishes One Roof is their integrated "one roof" approach—combining purchase, education, renovation, and rental resources under a single organization rather than referring clients elsewhere. Their community land trust model provides permanent affordability by separating land ownership from home ownership, preventing displacement and enabling generational wealth building for low-income households. They explicitly market themselves as a trusted resource that "works hard to say yes" by meeting people where they are and identifying appropriate options.

One Roof is genuinely a non-profit free-help resource appropriate for low-income homebuyers and homeowners, though their services appear geographically limited to their specific service area. The organization relies on donations and partnerships rather than charging fees for core counseling and education services, making them accessible to underserved populations. However, website content lacks specific details about service area coverage, waitlists, income limits, or measurable outcomes.
pros: Free homebuyer education programs explicitly designed to prepare first-time homebuyers for successful home purchases
Community Land Trust model creates permanently affordable housing that prevents displacement and builds equity for low-income families
Affordable home improvement loans available at virtually any income level, with flexible underwriting
Free community tool lending library open to all residents without charge or membership requirements
Tenant-landlord mediation and education services to reduce housing evictions in the community
Integrated one-stop-shop approach combining purchase, education, renovation, and rental support
Ongoing stewardship and support for CLT homeowners post-purchase to ensure long-term success
cons: Website does not specify geographic service area, making it unclear if this organization serves your location
No information about income limits, eligibility requirements, or CLT home purchase price ranges on main website
Limited details about homebuyer education program structure, duration, or curriculum content
No transparency regarding down payment assistance amounts, loan terms for repair/remodel loans, or program wait times
Website lacks contact information, office hours, or how to apply for programs beyond generic donation/mailing list signup
best_for: Low-income first-time homebuyers seeking free education and CLT home purchase opportunities in their service area
Current homeowners needing affordable financing for home repairs and renovations without high income requirements
Renters and landlords experiencing disputes who need mediation and education to improve tenant-landlord relationships
diagnosis: Best for low-income individuals and families in their service area seeking affordable homeownership through a community land trust model, free homebuyer education, or help with home repairs and rental housing issues. Main caveat: website lacks critical details about service area, eligibility, and program specifics—contact the organization directly to determine if you qualify and what programs are available to you.
services: Community Land Trust home purchases and listings
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
